rationale: clauses: controlled index document(KILLED: index frontmatter id DMR-001->DMR-999 fails the self-entry frontmatter-agreement assertion — the index lists itself in dmr.yml; frontmatter presence also independently enforced by the DI-2 test over documents/*.md), rendered from repository data(KILLED: jinja loop {%- for entry in dmr.entries %} replaced by hand-written table rows — index_src loop assertion fails; the "no {%- for in rendered" assertion guards against unrendered output), lists each controlled document with its identity and revision(KILLED: dmr.yml SOP-DC-001 revision 1->2 disagrees with the document frontmatter; KILLED: dmr.yml entry id SOP-DC-001->SOP-DC-999 fails frontmatter agreement). NOT COVERED: enumerating the specification set — deleting the entire SOP-DC-001 entry from data/dmr.yml SURVIVED; the test iterates whatever entries dmr.yml declares (per-entry presence + consistency) but asserts nothing about completeness, i.e. that every controlled document under documents/ appears in the index.
uncovered_clauses: enumerating the specification set (completeness): removing a controlled document entry from data/dmr.yml passes — no assertion that the index covers all controlled documents
